transcript: Hello everyone. Hope everyone's doing well. Incredible day one of Breakpoint and it's truly an honor to be here uh speaking and unveiling the the rollout of Lightspeed. So Lightspeed is the industry's first investor relations platform built and designed for Solana investors. Um, as the industry grows and matures, so do the participants within it within it and therefore the way we need to communicate with those investors. Uh the the new wave of participants is really coming with a background and expectations around how they expect companies to be communicating with them, whether that's in uh the consistency at which that happens or the cadence and the access to information around uh the financial health of the business or the core KPIs and how they're tracking with their growth towards those goals. Um and the Solana Foundation wanted to deliver on exactly that in a differentiated and unique way uh and in a very powerful way with the rollout of Lightspeed, which will help enable the that communication. Uh I think to answer the question of why now, uh I think it's very important to set set some context around the evolution of the crypto industry. And I think it kind of rolls into three distinct phases where the first of which being the sort of the bootstrapping phase. I think this is sort of hallmarked by the 2017 ICO era. This was like very retail driven, a ton of fun uh anybody who remembers it had a great time, anonymous founders, white papers and a dream. And while this was a ton of fun, it was largely uninvestable to institutional capital or serious capital in any capacity. And the transition into the scaling phase or phase two really occurred uh in 2019 2020 when venture capital started funding these new and exciting ideas of the prospect of building on a globally distributed system and putting software on that that enabled uh developers to get instant distribution to everybody in the world with nothing more than a smartphone and an internet connection. And that's kind of the dream that a lot of us uh came to love. And we really started to see uh exchanges and custody solutions start to professionalize and evolve uh and enable that serious capital to get involved. And as those early waves of uh projects started to see some some levels of success and start to test product market fit, uh we start to see the evolution to phase three which is where we're at today where these businesses are generating real revenue and doing so profitably uh and we're starting to see the the next leg of mainstream integration start to happen where it's every day especially today we've heard a whole uh onslaught of of several announcements around banks and large fintechs coming and building products on chain. And so when the marginal flows are no longer being driven by retail but rather institutional investors that have these expectations of how to be communicated with when it comes to investor relations. It's now funds and pensions and asset managers and asset issuers. And so that is what Lightspeed sets out to deliver. Uh and so not only for Solana and SOL the asset but also for the broader ecosystem but focusing on on the first piece there we're covering the financials of sol uh of Solana of the Solana ecosystem right? And so understanding what how network revenue is generated. What is the composition of that? How is it growing? What are the growth drivers there and available to the to the ecosystem? Um the on-chain activity. What is driving transaction activity and TPS? What do those transactions consist of? How would you categorize them? Um stablecoins. How are they growing? What types of stablecoins are on chain? Where are they being used? DEXs and DEX aggregators. Um RWAs and lending is where we're building towards as well. So getting this sector by sector view and understanding of what is happening on chain. Um and not only for Solana but also SOL the asset. So the the core drivers uh of the supply change and staking rates are very important uh to understanding uh the asset itself and that data will be available here as well. But zooming out beyond just SOL and SOL the asset and Solana the chain, uh there's a broader ecosystem of applications being built on chain. So we'll have full data coverage of Solana and SOL tokenomics as well as multi-product DeFi protocols like Jupiter or DePIN networks like GEODNET and Helium. Or the top Solana prop AMMs like Meteora and Phoenix as well as traditional DEXs like Raydium and Orca. All of this data coming into one spot so investors have this full understanding of not only what's happening on chain but within the broader ecosystem. And as these these things grow and mature, so will the data coverage that we supply. So really what I'm personally excited about is building out coverage of lending markets and vaults and liquid staking and RWAs. Um we're rolling out our stablecoins view uh tomorrow uh uh Eastern time I'm all messed up on which time zone I'm in. Uh and not only will that data exist and be available to all the investors within this platform but it will be importantly contextualized within reports and recent news coverage. Um so the best way to stay on top of what is happening on Solana is a Blockworks Research monthly note which will now be available in within this platform to help people understand uh the key growth drivers the core KPIs and the important upcoming uh network level changes that will impact those those figures. Uh and additionally quarterly reports for Solana native protocols will also be available within the platform. Uh so when these these teams are now doing a better job of communicating to that investor group at that regular cadence uh that like such as Raydium and Helium that will now be available within this platform which puts everything the data and the contextualization in one spot for investors to fully understand the Solana ecosystem. So the product launches in Q1 and we're really focused on serving this for liquid token funds institutional capital managers or asset managers um asset issuers and large individual token holders. And so we encourage you to apply so head over to lightspeed.vip to apply today. Thank you.

[Company](https://www.lightspeedhq.com/news/cat/company/)# Lightspeed Announces Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results and Raises Outlook for Fiscal 2026
* 2025-11-06
minute read
##### Share
* []()
* []()
* []()
![]()
*Revenue of $319.0 million grew 15% year-over-year and exceeded outlook*
*Gross profit margins improved to 42% with gross profit increasing 18% year-over-year, exceeding outlook*
*Positive cash flow from operating activities of $25.5 million, and Adjusted Free Cash Flow (1) of $18.0 million*
*Across retail in North America and hospitality in Europe, Lightspeed added \~2,000 net Customer Locations in the quarter*
*Across retail in North America and hospitality in Europe, GTV grew 15% year-over-year*
**Lightspeed reports in US dollars and in accordance with IFRS Accounting Standards**.
Lightspeed Commerce Inc., the unified omnichannel platform powering ambitious retail and hospitality businesses in over 100 countries, today announced financial results for the three and six months ended September 30, 2025.
“Lightspeed’s second consecutive quarter of outperformance demonstrates that our transformation is delivering as planned,” said Dax Dasilva, Founder and CEO. “Our AI-driven innovation, bold go-to-market strategy, and disciplined execution are fueling profitable growth, and we’re just getting started.”
“Lightspeed delivered another exceptional quarter, with revenue, gross profit growth and Adjusted EBITDA all exceeding outlook,” said Asha Bakshani, CFO. “Our renewed strategic focus is allowing us to scale at an accelerated pace and the significant growth in Adjusted Free Cash Flow*(1)*from $1.6 million a year ago to $18 million this quarter demonstrates our ability to invest for growth while continuing to strengthen profitability.”
*(1) Non-IFRS measure or ratio. See the section entitled “Non-IFRS Measures and Ratios” and the reconciliation to the most directly comparable IFRS measure or ratio.*
**Second Quarter Financial Highlights**
(All comparisons are relative to the three-month period ended September 30, 2024 unless otherwise stated):
* Total revenue of $319.0 million, an increase of 15% year-over-year.
* Transaction-based revenue of $215.8 million, an increase of 17% year-over-year.
* Subscription revenue of $93.5 million, an increase of 9% year-over-year.
* Net loss of ($32.7) million, or ($0.24) per share, as compared to a net loss of ($29.7) million, or ($0.19) per share.
* After adjusting for certain items, such as share-based compensation, the Company delivered Adjusted Income1 of $22.2 million, or $0.16 per share*(1)*, as compared to Adjusted Income*(1)*of $19.9 million, or $0.13 per share1 . •Adjusted EBITDA1 of $21.3 million up from Adjusted EBITDA1 of $14.0 million.
* Cash flows from operating activities of $25.5 million as compared to cash flows used in operating activities of ($11.3) million, and Adjusted Free Cash Flow*(1)*of $18.0 million as compared to Adjusted Free Cash Flow1 of $1.6 million.
* As at September 30, 2025, Lightspeed had $462.5 million in cash and cash equivalents.
![](https://assets.lightspeedhq.com/img/4cc1fcdb-1.jpg)
**Second Quarter Operational Highlights**
* Lightspeed delivered several new product releases in the quarter including:
* Multiple AI related offerings within retail such as:**AI Showroom**, which provides retailers a hassle-free AI powered online presence for customers not ready to commit to a full e-commerce experience;**AI-driven web builder tool**moved from beta to general availability; and**AI-gene

# [![Solana Company - Main - Black.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/1986b97e-8656-4213-9218-ae51324d696d?size=3)](https://www.solanacompany.co/)
# Solana Company (NASDAQ: HSDT) Releases Investor Update
November 03, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Solana Company](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Solana%20Company)FollowSolana Company
Share
* * * * * * * * NEWTOWN, Pa., Nov. 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Solana Company (NASDAQ: HSDT) (the “Company” or “HSDT”), a publicly listed digital asset treasury dedicated to acquiring and holding Solana (SOL), today announced the release of its latest corporate presentation (the “Investor Update”) and the election of Cosmo Jiang, General Partner at Pantera Capital, to the Board of Directors (the “Board”).
The Investor Update highlights Solana’s accelerating adoption across real-world asset tokenization, decentralized finance, and payments. The network now leads all Layer 1 blockchains in decentralized exchange (DEX) trading volume and asset issuance market share, with over 23 billion transactions year-to-date and 2.3 million active wallets. Stablecoin transaction volume on Solana has also reached record levels, supported by integrations with PayPal, Stripe and Western Union, while Solana’s stablecoin market cap currently sits at over $14.5B.
Solana Company continues to advance its mission to maximize SOL per share through ecosystem advocacy, disciplined accumulation, and active treasury management with institutional-grade staking. The Company remains one of the largest Solana-focused digital asset treasuries globally, operating with long-term alignment to the Solana ecosystem.
“Our strategy remains grounded in transparency, conviction, and disciplined execution,” said Cosmo Jiang, General Partner at Pantera Capital and Board Director at Solana Company. “I am honored to be elected to the Board and will work together with HSDT’s management to refine our focus on compounding SOL by holding and capturing sustainable on-chain yield in a secure, institutional framework.”
“Congratulations to Cosmo on his election to the Board. In a period of market adjustment, our strategic focus remains unwavering, as does our commitment to delivering value to our shareholders,” said Joseph Chee, Executive Chairman of Solana Company and Chairman of Summer Capital. “Adding Cosmo’s experience building successful DATs to our Board will boost our effort to make Solana Company the institutional-grade choice for access to SOL, the blockchain of choice for the future financial system.”
The full Investor Update can be viewed at: www.solanacompany.co/about
**Forward-Looking Statements**
Thispress releasecontains statements that constitute “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the U.S. federal securities laws. There can be no assurance that such statements will prove to be accurate and actual results and future events could differ materially from those expressed or implied by such statements. tags.impact_reason: Lightspeed is a useful tool for improving investor relations within the Solana ecosystem, suggesting a move towards greater institutional involvement, but its impact is not immediately ecosystem-shifting.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Crystalline Prism as the Semantic Anchor because the product 'Lightspeed' takes raw, complex data (a single beam of white light) and organizes it into clear, standardized, institutional-grade reporting (a structured spectrum of colored light). This perfectly visualizes the core function of the IR platform. The prism itself feels solid, precise, and high-value, aligning with the 'Institutional' tag. For the integrated text, I chose 'PHASE 3', a key concept from the transcript defining this new, mature era for crypto investment. It's integrated as a holographic data point within the light beam, making it a diegetic part of the technological process.
visual_idea.description: A large, flawlessly clear crystalline prism is suspended in a dark, minimalist environment. From the top left, a single, brilliant beam of white light—representing raw Solana data—strikes the prism. As it passes through, it refracts into an organized, fanning spectrum of glowing, holographic data streams. These streams are not just colors, but visualized financial charts and network graphs in Solana's green and purple palette. Just before entering the prism, the diegetic text 'PHASE 3' is visible as a shimmering holographic node within the white light beam itself. The background is a deep, out-of-focus server room, hinting at immense computational power.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, flawlessly clear crystalline prism, massive in scale, is suspended with an epic sense of gravitas in a dark, minimalist environment. Emphasizing verticality and negative space above, the prism dominates the frame. From the top-left, a single, brilliant beam of intense white light, representing raw Solana data, strikes the prism. Within this powerful white light beam, just before it enters the prism, the characters 'PHASE 3' are visible as a shimmering, glowing holographic node, intricately woven into the light itself. As the light beam passes through the prism, it dramatically refracts into an organized, fanning spectrum of vibrant, glowing holographic data streams. These streams are not merely colors but are precisely visualized financial charts and complex network graphs, rendered distinctly in Solana's signature green and purple palette. The deep, out-of-focus background reveals the subtle blur of an institutional server room, hinting at immense computational power. The scene is illuminated with soft commercial product lighting featuring global illumination and a neutral white (5500K) key light, enhancing the crystalline clarity and holographic luminescence through ray-traced optics and physically based rendering (PBR). This creates a premium, high-tech aesthetic with even light distribution and subtle ambient occlusion. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, cinematic lighting, institutional tech aesthetic. --ar 3:4

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Crystalline Prism as the Semantic Anchor because the product 'Lightspeed' takes raw, complex data (a single beam of white light) and organizes it into clear, standardized, institutional-grade reporting (a structured spectrum of colored light). This perfectly visualizes the core function of the IR platform. The prism itself feels solid, precise, and high-value, aligning with the 'Institutional' tag. For the integrated text, I chose 'PHASE 3', a key concept from the transcript defining this new, mature era for crypto investment. visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on a sharp facet of the crystalline prism. We see the precise moment the concentrated beam of white light enters the crystal, beginning to split and refract into intricate filaments of colored holographic data. The focus is on the mesmerizing internal complexity of the light's transformation and the flawless, polished texture of the crystal. The image captures the essence of clarification and analysis at a micro level. NO TEXT.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A symmetrical, centrally focused macro shot, perfectly composed for a 1:1 aspect ratio, filling the frame with intricate texture. The image captures the precise moment a concentrated beam of pristine white light enters a sharp facet of a flawless, polished crystalline prism. The light immediately begins to split and refract into mesmerizing, intricate filaments of colored holographic data, showcasing its internal complexity. The crystal's texture is impeccably rendered, highlighting its clarity and perfection, emphasizing clarification and analysis at a micro level. The entire scene is bathed in clean, even, soft commercial product lighting with neutral white (5500K) global illumination, creating subtle, soft gradient shadows and ambient occlusion. The lighting emphasizes the high dynamic range and photorealistic qualities. This is a product/technical photography style with high contrast, meticulously rendered to 8k resolution. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic.

editorial.headline: Blockworks and Solana Foundation Unveil 'Lightspeed' Institutional IR Platform
editorial.standfirst: As crypto enters its profitable 'Phase 3,' institutional investors demand equity-grade reporting. Lightspeed answers the call with standardized data and research for the Solana ecosystem.
key_takeaways: Strategic partnership between **Blockworks** and **Solana Foundation** to professionalize investor relations.
Platform launches in Q1, targeting asset managers, liquid funds, and pension funds.
Coverage extends beyond SOL to include protocols like **Jupiter**, **Helium**, and **Raydium**.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The crypto industry is transitioning from its speculative "bootstrapping" phase into a mature "Phase 3" defined by real revenue and institutional participation. **Dan Smith** of **Blockworks** notes that while the ICO era was retail-driven and the scaling era was VC-funded, the current landscape requires a new communication standard. Institutional allocators—pension funds and asset managers—cannot rely on fragmented Discord updates or Twitter threads to underwrite multi-million dollar positions.

To bridge this gap, **Blockworks** and the **Solana Foundation** have unveiled **Lightspeed**, a dedicated investor relations platform launching in Q1. The hub provides a unified view of **Solana**'s financial health, tracking network revenue, supply dynamics, and staking rates. Crucially, it moves beyond the base layer to offer deep dives into sector-specific drivers, from stablecoin velocity and DEX volume to RWA adoption.

The platform's value proposition lies in its coverage of the broader application layer. Investors will access standardized reporting for multi-product DeFi protocols like **Jupiter**, DePIN networks like **Helium** and **GEODNET**, and liquidity venues like **Meteora** and **Raydium**. By integrating **Blockworks Research** monthly notes and quarterly protocol reports directly into the dashboard at `lightspeed.vip`, **Lightspeed** aims to contextualize raw on-chain data, offering the "equity-like" clarity required for serious capital deployment.
